title: Gandahati Waterfall
tagline: Gandahati Waterfall — heritage city of Odisha

overview.short: The Gandahati waterfall is located on the Mahendratanaya River in the Gajapati District in Southern Odisha, India.
overview.description: The Gandahati waterfall is located on the Mahendratanaya River in the Gajapati District in Southern Odisha, India. It is a popular scenic attraction located about 30 km from Paralakhemundi. Gandahati waterfall is a tourist destination in the Gajapati District of Odisha and attracts a large number of visitors during winter. This waterfall is 153 km from Berhampur and 30km from Paralakhemundi.

topPlaces.name: Gatabandha
topPlaces.description: Gatabandha is a village of Koksara Block in Dharmagarh Sub-Division in Kalahandi District of Odisha State, India. This village comes under Phupagaon Panchayat of Kokasara Tehasil in Kalahandi District.

items.note: Gatabandha is a village of Koksara Block in Dharmagarh Sub-Division in Kalahandi District of Odisha State, India. This village comes under Phupagaon Panchayat of Kokasara Tehasil in Kalahandi District.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Gandahati Waterfall?
faq.a: The best time to visit Gandahati Waterfall is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Gandahati Waterfall?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Visakhapatnam Airport (170 km) and the nearest railway station is Visakhapatnam Junction (165 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Gandahati Waterfall?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Gandahati Waterfall at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Gandahati Waterfall?
faq.a: Stays in Gandahati Waterfall start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.
